channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Top Trump administration officials, including “border czar” Tom Homan and the acting deputy attorney general, visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcementin the nation’s third-largest city as federal agencies touted arrests around the country.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Settlement reached in lawsuit involving HGTV stars' companies: court records
item.description: A settlement has been reached in the lawsuit involving an Arkansas-based couple and stars of HGTV's "Fixer to Fabulous" just days before the parties were set to go to trial.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, Fox 5

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A Washington state woman has been charged in the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont, which happened days after authorities began watching her and a German companion who also died in the roadside shootout, the FBI said Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump's attempt to end birthright citizenship would overturn more than a century of precedent
item.description: President Donald Trump has said since his first administration that he wants to end birthright citizenship, a constitutional right for everyone born in the United States. This week he issued an executive order that would eliminate it, upending more than a century of precedent.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM LEE BREWER and JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance breaks tie to confirm Pete Hegseth as Defense secretary
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday broke a Senate tie to confirm Pete Hegseth as President Trump's secretary of Defense, capping a bruising two-month fight over the nominee, who faced a litany of allegations that included sexual misconduct, financial mismanagement and excessive drinking. GOP Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Trump visit is 'morale boost': CA fire official
item.description: "I don't know what Trump can give us, but it's a morale boost," said Cal Fire battalion chief Brent Pascua.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas Raiders agree to hire 73-year-old Pete Carroll as their head coach, AP source says
item.description: Pete Carroll has a youthful energy that many people half his age don't possess, and the Raiders hope that enthusiasm — and, more importantly, his proven track record — translates to Las Vegas.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI and MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Man says he was behind some of the viral googly eyes on public art in Oregon
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — A resident of the central Oregon city of Bend says he was the person behind some of the googly eyes that appeared on sculptures around the city in recent months and sparked a viral sensation widely covered by news outlets.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump pumps coal as answer to AI power needs but any boost could be short-lived
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyo. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s promise to go all in on fossil fuels includes praise for coal, a reliable but polluting energy source that's long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump cuts Dr. Fauci's security detail
item.description: Asked if he would feel partially responsible if something were to happen to Fauci, Trump said he would not.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Defense Department orders 'pause' of social media accounts
item.description: The Defense Department said it needs time to review its social media programming to match Trump's priorities.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Florida man pleads guilty to assaulting American Airlines gate agent
item.description: A Florida man pleaded guilty in federal court after he assaulted an American Airlines gate agent at Dallas-Fort Worth International Airport, according to online court documents.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Costco stands by its DEI policies as other companies scale back
item.description: Costco is pushing back on a shareholder proposal that urges the wholesale club operator to conduct an evaluation of any business risks posed by its diversity, equity and inclusion practices. Investors were expected to vote on the recommendation during the company's annual meeting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The man pushed onto New York's subway tracks says he will ride again
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man who survived being shoved onto subway tracks ahead of an oncoming train said that in spite of the physical and psychological trauma, he eventually plans to make his way back to the train.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oklahoma wants federal inmate transferred so he can be put to death
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Oklahoma's top prosecutor asked the federal Bureau of Prisons to transfer an inmate to state custody so that he could be executed for his role in the kidnapping and killing of a 77-year-old woman in 1999.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Marilyn Manson won't face domestic violence and sexual assault charges, DA says
item.description: "We recognize and applaud the courage and resilience of the women who came forward to make reports and share their experiences, and we thank them for their cooperation and patience with the investigation," the district attorney's office said in a news release explaining the decision not to pursue ch
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, Fox 5

item.title: Target is ending its diversity goals as a strong DEI opponent occupies the White House
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Discount store chain Target said Friday that it would join rival Walmart and a number of other prominent American brands in scaling back diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives that have come under attack from conservative activists and, as of this week, the White House.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: ICE agents raid New Jersey seafood store, detain US military veteran
item.description: The store owner told NewsNation affiliate WPIX that a U.S. military veteran was among the people detained.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Spirit Airlines passengers can now be removed from flight for 'offensive' tattoos, clothing
item.description: Passengers flying on Spirit Airlines could now be removed from a flight if a tattoo or piece of clothing is deemed offensive, according to the airline's updated policy.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Progress is made on a huge fire north of Los Angeles while new fires erupt in Southern California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Evacuation orders were lifted Thursday for tens of thousands as firefighters with air support slowed the spread of a huge wildfire churning through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles, but new blazes erupted in San Diego County, briefly triggering more evacuations.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 2025 Oscar nominations: The biggest snubs and surprises
item.description: The members of the film academy voted to honor a few outliers — and overlook a couple of hopeful contenders — at the 97th Academy Awards.
item.dc:creator: Lindsey Bahr, Fox 5

item.title: Trump pulls security for Pompeo, others amid Iran threats
item.description: President Trump pulled security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, both of whom have faced threats from Iran amid their hard-line positions toward the Islamic Republic, multiple outlets reported on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Over 60M kids' data may have been stolen: What you need to know
item.description: More than 62 million students and 9.5 million teachers may have had their data stolen, according to a new report.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, Fox 5

item.title: Resale prices for iPhones 'with TikTok installed' soar up to $50,000
item.description: TikTok is no longer available in the App Store as of Thursday, however there's still a way to get back on the platform – for a price.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Convicted killer sings in front of victims' families
item.description: A man convicted of murdering two women in 1986 interrupted his own sentencing hearing on Tuesday by singing in front of his victims' families.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WGN 9

item.title: Trump’s Jan. 6 pardons divide House Republicans
item.description: President Trump’s decision to pardon hundreds of protesters who stormed the Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021 — including many accused of assaulting police officers — is dividing House Republicans, with some lawmakers touting the day-one decree and others criticizing it as ill-advised.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gulf of Whatnow? Trump's geographic renaming plans leave mapmakers pondering what to do next
item.description: What's in a name change, after all? The water bordered by the Southern United States, Mexico and Cuba will be critical to shipping lanes and vacationers whether it’s called the Gulf of Mexico, as it has been for four centuries, or the Gulf of America, as President Donald Trump ordered this week.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 12-year-old boy shot after snowball hit car, police say
item.description: A 12-year-old boy was shot while throwing snowballs Wednesday evening in Hartford, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Wall of ice the size of Rhode Island heading toward penguin-packed island off Antarctica
item.description: The world's biggest iceberg — a wall of ice the size of Rhode Island — is lumbering toward a remote island off Antarctica that's home to millions of penguins and seals.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Experts worry that Trump's Jan. 6 pardons will legitimize political violence, embolden extremists
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After President Donald Trump pardoned around 1,500 Jan. 6 Capitol rioters on Monday, far-right activists cheered the move and said it strengthened their loyalty to him. Some also borrowed from the president’s own rhetoric, calling for retribution.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump won't ban immigration arrests at churches. Now clergy are weighing how to resist
item.description: Numerous faith leaders across the U.S. say the immigration crackdown launched by President Donald Trump’s new administration has sown fear within their migrant-friendly congregations.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, LUIS ANDRES HENAO and DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sen. Lisa Murkowski to vote against Hegseth; first Republican to oppose a Trump Cabinet pick
item.description: Sen. Lisa Murkowski announced Thursday that she will vote against confirming Pete Hegseth to lead Pentagon, becoming the first Republican to oppose one of President Donald Trump's Cabinet picks.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 6 planets to align in the night sky: Here's when
item.description: You might want to keep your eyes on the skies through next month: Six planets will align in January and February.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s blizzard of orders gets pushback, questions from GOP lawmakers
item.description: President Donald Trump’s blizzard of executive orders during the first few days of his presidency has sent Republican lawmakers scrambling to make sense of what impact they’ll have on the country, and some GOP senators are already raising questions and concerns.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs sues man who claimed to have incriminating sex tapes, says they didn't exist
item.description: Combs, 55, has pleaded not guilty to sex trafficking charges lodged against him after his September arrest.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9
